description: Discount note pricer for repos, commercial paper, and short-term interest-bearing instruments. Prices any money-market instrument whose value is a single maturity cash flow discounted back using a risk-free curve plus a credit z-spread. Supports both zero-coupon (discount) and simple-interest (interest_bearing) payoff structures.

required_fields.name: issue_date
required_fields.description: Start date of the instrument — repo open leg date for repos (DD-MM-YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD)

required_fields.name: face_value
required_fields.description: Face / redemption amount (e.g. 1000000). For repos: initial cash lent or borrowed.

optional_fields.name: coupon_rate
optional_fields.description: Annualised rate for interest_bearing instruments (decimal or %). For repos: the repo rate. Maturity CF = face_value × (1 + coupon_rate × t).

optional_fields.name: day_count
optional_fields.description: Day count convention for time fraction and money-market yield calculations

optional_fields.name: credit_spread_bp
optional_fields.description: Z-spread over the discount curve in basis points (issuer credit premium)

optional_fields.name: settlement_days
optional_fields.description: Days to settlement. Default 0 (same-day, standard money-market convention). Use 2 for T+2 markets.

optional_fields.name: calendar
optional_fields.description: Holiday calendar for settlement date adjustment

optional_fields.name: description
optional_fields.description: Human-readable name of the instrument (e.g. 'Apple CP 3M' or 'GC Repo EUR O/N')

optional_fields.name: currency
optional_fields.description: Settlement currency (e.g. EUR, USD) — used for curve selection

optional_fields.name: repo_collateral
optional_fields.description: Collateral details for repo instruments (informational only — not used in pricing)

fields.name: instrument_id
fields.description: ISIN or identifier of the collateral security

fields.name: market_value
fields.description: Current dirty market value of the collateral

fields.name: haircut_pct
fields.description: Haircut percentage applied to collateral value (e.g. 2.0 for 2%)
